turns.zh: 嗯，你看，我是个乐观主义者，也是个可能玩不了这个游戏的人，因为我更看重高道德标准、高伦理。当我说“你将成为我的合作伙伴”，你就是我的合作伙伴，我们一起做事，而不是像 OpenAI 那样“我要和所有人合作，我们是排他的，但其实不是”，你知道，这有点像今天的社交媒体数据约会套路，对吧？所以，我觉得，我不知道，我总在问自己：如果史蒂夫·乔布斯在这里，他会如何应对这个环境？

turns.en: Well, would he? I don't have an answer. Do you think he would be in the White House? You sort of have to...
turns.zh: 嗯，他会吗？我没有答案。你觉得他会进白宫吗？你有点不得不……

turns.en: I have no idea. I think Gavin Newsom said something fairly charitable about Tim Cook, which is sort of, you know, he has to play the game. But yeah, it's hard to know if Steve Jobs would have, you know, knowing what I know and working alongside him for a decade, I don't think he would have played, but I don't know. I played along, but I don't know.
turns.zh: 我不知道。我觉得加文·纽森对蒂姆·库克说了些相当宽容的话，大意是他必须玩这个游戏。但嗯，很难说史蒂夫·乔布斯会不会，你知道，以我对他的了解，以及和他共事十年的经历，我不认为他会玩，但我不确定。我跟着玩了，但我不确定。

turns.en: But no, really, I think that, you know, I think Dario is doing a great job. He got in front of Davos saying, you know, this is BS, this is not right, this is not right. Dario got up and saying LLMs aren't the thing, you know, and there's more to come. So, I think we need more leaders like that because we have too many, you know, people with questionable value systems, running these things and, you know, I wish they were there or they were more vocal, right? Because Tim's a great guy, right? Tim's got high values, you know.
turns.zh: 但说真的，我觉得，你知道，我认为 Dario 做得很好。他在达沃斯站出来说，这全是胡扯，这不对，这不对。Dario 站起来说 LLM 不是关键，还有更多东西要来。所以，我觉得我们需要更多这样的领导者，因为太多价值观有问题的人在运营这些东西，我希望他们能站出来，或者更直言不讳，对吧？因为蒂姆是个好人，对吧？蒂姆有很高的价值观。

turns.en: I mean I agree with you. I mean Dario, they just Anthropic just released a constitution for flawed where it's like they're being extremely thoughtful and I tweeted something. It's like in a moment of anti-intellectualism here they are it's like let's have some philosophy PhDs academics you know think through you know within a real principled way like I really admire what they're doing.
turns.zh: 我同意你。Dario 他们，Anthropic 刚刚发布了一部针对缺陷的宪法，他们非常深思熟虑，我发了条推文。在反智主义盛行的时刻，他们却让哲学博士、学者们以真正有原则的方式思考，我真的很钦佩他们的做法。

turns.en: I think when a lot of investors and a lot of people get burned by this thing that we're in right now, there's going to be like a lot of naval gazing going 'oh you know what did we do right? what did we do wrong?' You're always going to have that Wall Street wolf full of Wall Street mentality and everybody's playing. But I remember that in '89, too, when it all fell out and then great companies like Google really came into play, right? So, we'll see what happens. I don't know.
turns.zh: 我觉得当很多投资者和很多人被我们现在所处的这个局面烧伤时，会有很多自我反省，比如“哦，我们做对了什么？做错了什么？”你总是会有那种华尔街狼的心态，每个人都在玩。但我也记得 89 年，当一切崩溃时，像谷歌这样的伟大公司才真正登场，对吧？所以，我们等着瞧吧。我不知道。

ts.sec: Advice for Young Engineers on Devices

turns.en: This has been a great conversation. Last one, last question. We've touched on a lot of devices. I mean, if you were, you know, I don't know, this smart engineer kid out of college like I want to make my name in devices, like where would you point him or her? Like what is the sort of device area that said okay make a bet on this for the next two decades?
turns.zh: 这次对话很棒。最后一个问题。我们谈了很多设备。如果你是一个刚毕业的聪明工程师，想在设备领域成名，你会建议他或她往哪个方向？哪个设备领域值得押注未来二十年？

turns.en: Understand the application really really well and understand if you even need hardware. See, we have more than enough hardware and all kinds of different things. It's going to be a federation of things with a really smart software thing. So don't think of just mechanical and devices, you got to think system and you got to think federated of devices to really get the next level of what it's going to happen. All this other stuff is all kind of features, not a product.
turns.zh: 非常非常深入地理解应用场景，并理解你是否真的需要硬件。你看，我们有足够多的硬件和各种不同的东西。未来将是一个由非常智能的软件连接起来的设备联邦。所以不要只考虑机械和设备，你必须考虑系统，考虑设备的联邦，才能真正达到下一个层次。其他所有东西都只是功能，不是产品。

turns.en: What I know I said that was the last question. I have to like I love my iPhone. I use it all the time. But don't you get we get sick of looking at it or it's like you're looking at the phone too much. Do you think in two decades like we're still going to be using like a here?
turns.zh: 我知道我说过这是最后一个问题。但我得说，我爱我的 iPhone，我一直在用。但你不觉得我们看它看得太多了吗？你觉得二十年后我们还会用这样的东西吗？

turns.en: We'll just put these in our ears and then when we put the phone in our pocket, it'll know the context and it'll just start talking to us and then more listening, less screen time. Yeah. You know, are there going to be different forms? These are open ear, over the ear, in ear. The noise cancellation is great. Okay, great. You're going to have the glasses. So, we have enough of the forms. They need to be pulled together with smarter software and better things to get them to do what they need.
turns.zh: 我们只需把这些戴在耳朵里，当手机放进口袋时，它会知道上下文，然后开始和我们对话，更多聆听，更少屏幕时间。是的。会有不同的形态吗？这些是开放式、耳罩式、入耳式。降噪效果很好。好的，很好。还会有眼镜。所以，形态已经足够多了。它们需要用更智能的软件和更好的东西整合起来，才能完成它们需要做的事。

turns.en: You know, I'm not saying... what headphones do you use?
turns.zh: 我不是说……你用什么耳机？

turns.en: These are Nothings. Okay. These are Nothing. I got the over the ear, the over the headphone ones. Yeah. And also the opens. I love them. Absolutely love them.
turns.zh: 这些是 Nothing 的。好的。这是 Nothing。我有耳罩式的，头戴式的。还有开放式。我爱它们。绝对爱。

turns.en: Tony Fadell, this has been awesome. Thanks so much for doing this with me.
turns.zh: Tony Fadell，太棒了。非常感谢你和我做这个节目。

turns.en: Yeah. Great. Thanks. Thanks a lot, Eric.
turns.zh: 嗯。很好。谢谢。非常感谢，Eric。

turns.en: Thank you for tuning in to this week's episode of the podcast. If you're new here, please like and subscribe. It really helps out the channel. Listen in for new episodes every week wherever you get your podcasts.
turns.zh: 感谢收听本周的播客节目。如果你是新来的，请点赞和订阅。这对频道很有帮助。每周在您获取播客的地方收听新剧集。

consensus.en: AI needs context from sensors; future devices will be companions to smartphones, not replacements.
consensus.zh: AI需要来自传感器的上下文；未来设备将是智能手机的伴侣，而非替代品。

consensus.en: LLMs have fundamental problems like hallucination; context-specific models are needed for reliability.
consensus.zh: 大语言模型存在幻觉等根本性问题；需要特定上下文的模型才能保证可靠性。

consensus.en: Smart home is still 5-6 years away from mass-market vision due to infrastructure and cost issues.
consensus.zh: 由于基础设施和成本问题，智能家居距离大众市场愿景仍需5-6年。

consensus.en: OpenAI's strategy is 'too big to fail' with massive funding, but Google is best positioned to win AI.
consensus.zh: OpenAI的策略是‘大到不能倒’，但谷歌在AI领域最有优势。

consensus.en: Apple should redefine mobility with lightweight vehicles, not compete in traditional car market.
consensus.zh: 苹果应重新定义出行方式，推出轻型车辆，而非在传统汽车市场竞争。

consensus.en: Platforms are not businesses; applications are where value is captured in AI.
consensus.zh: 平台不是生意，应用才是AI领域创造价值的地方。

contrarian.en: Apple has never done marketing before; their AI-first phone marketing was egregiously misleading.
contrarian.zh: 苹果从未做过营销；其AI优先手机的宣传极具误导性。

contrarian.en: OpenAI's per-user economics worsen as they scale; they lose more money per user.
contrarian.zh: OpenAI的单位经济随规模扩大而恶化；每个用户亏损更多。

contrarian.en: The smart home market is unprofitable for most players; IKEA is best positioned due to low cost.
contrarian.zh: 智能家居市场对大多数玩家无利可图；宜家因低成本而最具优势。

contrarian.en: Apple should bring back the iPod as a distraction-free music device for nostalgic users.
contrarian.zh: 苹果应重新推出iPod，作为怀旧用户的无干扰音乐设备。

contrarian.en: VR is only viable for episodic B2B and gaming, not for living in the metaverse.
contrarian.zh: VR仅适用于间歇性B2B和游戏，而非生活在元宇宙中。

contrarian.en: Elon Musk's XAI and Tesla are a house of cards; SpaceX is the only solid business.
contrarian.zh: 埃隆·马斯克的xAI和特斯拉是纸牌屋；SpaceX是唯一稳固的业务。
